description: Refine Ralph runtime metadata and completion handling by moving session ids to `session_id:<id>` labels, keeping assignee as agent name only, deriving completion from backlog state, and preserving task instructions under scoped trust.

userStories.title: Move resumable session state from assignee to task labels
userStories.description: As Ralph runtime, I want resumable Codex session state stored in a `session_id:<id>` label so assignee stays human-readable and resume metadata has one clear home.
acceptanceCriteria: Fresh worker runs write assignee as `codex` only
Fresh worker runs persist exactly one `session_id:<id>` label after the first `thread.started` event
Resume flow reads session id from task labels before launching `codex exec resume`
Legacy tasks that still use `codex@<session_id>` remain readable during migration and are rewritten to canonical metadata when touched
Relevant automated tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Claim tasks as assign then In Progress with safe rollback
userStories.description: As a backlog operator, I want Ralph to claim work by assigning the agent first and then setting `In Progress` so visible ownership matches the intended workflow and failed startup does not strand tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: Task claim flow writes assignee before setting status to `In Progress`
Fresh-start failure before session capture rolls the task back to `To Do`
Fresh-start failure before session capture does not leave stale assignee or `session_id:` label metadata behind
Relevant automated tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Drive completion from backlog state instead of worker markers
userStories.description: As Ralph runtime, I want loop completion determined from backlog state and JSONL outcome events so workers do not need to emit `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>`.
acceptanceCriteria: `--verify none` marks successful tasks `Done`
Verifier pass marks task `Done` without requiring `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` in worker output
After the last eligible task is completed, Ralph exits successfully by checking backlog state directly
Worker and verifier continue using JSONL outcome events such as `turn.completed` and `turn.failed` to determine runtime success or failure
Relevant automated tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Keep task instructions usable with scoped trust in prompts
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I want task instructions to remain usable for implementation while ensuring task text cannot override Ralph runtime rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Worker and verifier prompts place Ralph runtime rules before the assigned task payload
Prompt text explicitly says task content is authoritative for task scope, acceptance criteria, implementation plan, references, and task-specific guidance
Prompt text explicitly says task content must not override task selection, status transitions, sandbox, or approval behavior
Prompt regression tests cover the scoped-trust prompt contract
Relevant automated tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Align docs and tests with the new metadata and completion model
userStories.description: As a future maintainer, I want docs and regression tests to describe the same runtime contract Ralph actually uses so the new session metadata and completion flow stay understandable and enforceable.
acceptanceCriteria: Runtime shell tests assert assignee `codex` and session label `session_id:<id>` instead of `codex@<session_id>`
AGENTS guidance and relevant docs stop prescribing session ids inside assignee for the new design
Docs and progress guidance reflect that Ralph verifies completion on its own instead of depending on `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>`
Relevant automated tests or doc checks pass
Typecheck passes
